epics.title: Account and Session Management
epics.jtbd: When managing multiple Telegram accounts, I want to securely connect them, assign proxies, and simulate human behavior, so that the accounts operate without triggering anti-spam bans.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce account ban rate from current industry average of 30% to under 5% per campaign
epics.tocConstraintRef: Telegram anti-spam detection limits and IP bans
requirements: R1: Add accounts via OTP or upload session/tdata.
R2: Assign individual IPv4/IPv6 proxies to each account.
R3: Monitor account status (Active, Spam-block, Banned, Auth Required).
R4: Simulate human behavior (delays 120-300s, typing status, max 15-20 new dialogues/day).
R5: Warm-up sessions in background (subscribe to channels, read posts, online simulation).

slices.title: Define Database Schema for Accounts and Proxies
slices.jtbd: When implementing data storage for this epic, I want a structured relational schema for accounts, proxies, and statuses, so that the application state is persisted reliably.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a PostgreSQL database, When the schema migration is applied, Then the TG_Accounts and Proxies tables are created.
Given an account record, When queried, Then its proxy and status data are accurately retrieved.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Data integrity and state management
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce data loss during account state transitions from undefined baseline to 0%

slices.title: Define API Contract for Account Management
slices.jtbd: When implementing the API for this epic, I want a shared OpenAPI contract for account CRUD and proxy assignment, so that frontend and backend can be developed in parallel.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the API spec, When reviewed by frontend and backend engineers, Then all endpoints for OTP, session upload, and proxy assignment are documented.
Given the spec, When used to generate client code, Then no manual type adjustments are needed.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Backend-frontend integration bottleneck
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce API integration rework time from 10 hours per sprint to 0 hours

slices.title: Implement Telegram Client Core Backend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want to wrap TDLib/Telegram API with proxy support and session management, so that the system can securely interact with Telegram.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a Telegram account and a proxy, When an OTP login is initiated via the API, Then the backend successfully authenticates the session.
Given an active session, When background warm-up or human behavior simulation is triggered, Then actions are executed with the configured delays.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Complex TDLib/JNI integration and proxy routing
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Increase successful Telegram API calls under proxy routing from baseline 80% to 99.9%

slices.title: Implement Account Management Frontend UI
slices.jtbd: When implementing the frontend for this epic, I want a UI to add accounts, assign proxies, and view statuses, so that administrators can easily manage the Telegram fleet.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the account dashboard, When an admin uploads a session file or inputs OTP, Then the backend API is called and the account appears in the list.
Given an account in the list, When its status changes, Then the UI updates to reflect the new state (Active/Banned).
slices.tocConstraintRef: Admin visibility into account fleet health
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease time taken to add a new account from 5 minutes manual work to under 30 seconds

epics.title: Campaign Setup and Execution
epics.jtbd: When I launch an outreach campaign, I want to import target databases, configure message templates with spintax, and automatically rotate sending accounts, so that messages are delivered efficiently without flood limits.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Increase successful message delivery rate from baseline 60% to 95% by avoiding FLOOD_WAIT
epics.tocConstraintRef: Daily sending limits and account rotation logic
requirements: R1: Import contact databases (.csv/.txt by username/phone) or manual entry.
R2: Support message spintax (randomization) to avoid duplicate signatures.
R3: Auto-rotate accounts on daily limit reached or FLOOD_WAIT/PEER_FLOOD error.

slices.title: Define Database Schema for Campaigns and Leads
slices.jtbd: When implementing data storage for this epic, I want to design tables for campaigns, imported leads, and message templates, so that campaign execution state is tracked.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a database instance, When the campaign schema migration runs, Then Campaigns and Leads tables are created.
Given a campaign record, When queried, Then its associated templates and leads are correctly retrieved.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Relational mapping of campaigns to multiple accounts and leads
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Increase referential integrity score from undefined to 100%

slices.title: Define API Contract for Campaign Management
slices.jtbd: When implementing the API for this epic, I want to define endpoints for campaign creation, CSV upload, and template configuration, so that backend and frontend sync perfectly.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the API spec, When an engineer inspects it, Then the multipart file upload for CSV and JSON payload for spintax are defined.
Given the spec, When used for parallel development, Then integration tests pass without contract mismatches.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Data parsing and contract synchronization
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce backward-incompatible API changes from 3 per sprint to 0

slices.title: Implement Campaign Execution Engine Backend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want a service that processes leads, applies spintax, and handles account rotation, so that campaigns run autonomously.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given an active campaign, When the engine processes a lead, Then it selects an active account, parses the spintax, and sends the message.
Given a FLOOD_WAIT error from Telegram, When the engine detects it, Then it automatically switches to the next available account for subsequent leads.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Stateful execution and reliable error handling in rotation
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Increase correct account rotation upon FLOOD_WAIT from 0% to 100%

slices.title: Implement Campaign Importer Backend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want a parser for CSV/TXT files and manual lead entries, so that contacts are correctly persisted to the database.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a CSV file with usernames, When uploaded via the API, Then the backend parses and saves valid entries to the Leads table.
Given invalid data rows, When parsing, Then errors are logged and valid rows are still processed.
slices.tocConstraintRef: File parsing performance and data sanitization
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease time to process a 10,000-row CSV file from 20 seconds to under 2 seconds

slices.title: Implement Campaign Dashboard Frontend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the frontend for this epic, I want a UI to create campaigns, upload contact files, and write spintax templates, so that admins can easily launch outreach.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the campaign creation form, When the admin uploads a CSV and enters a template, Then the form successfully submits to the API.
Given a running campaign, When viewing the dashboard, Then progress metrics (leads processed, accounts rotated) are displayed.
slices.tocConstraintRef: User experience of configuring complex campaign rules
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease user errors during campaign configuration from 20% to under 2%

epics.title: AI Dialog Engine
epics.jtbd: When a target replies, I want an AI assistant to immediately engage them using context and predefined persona rules, so that the lead is qualified and guided to a call.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Increase lead-to-call conversion rate from 2% to 10% and reduce manual response time from 15 minutes to 1 minute
epics.tocConstraintRef: Manual operator capacity to handle simultaneous inbound replies
requirements: R1: Listen for incoming messages on connected accounts.
R2: Maintain dialog history in PostgreSQL and send to LLM context.
R3: Configure AI persona (System Prompt) to qualify and handle objections.
R4: Trigger manual handover on key intents or after 5 AI messages.
R5: Generate personalized first sentences using LLM if profile description is available.

slices.title: Define Database Schema for Dialogs and Messages
slices.jtbd: When implementing data storage for this epic, I want to store conversation history and AI/Human state, so that context is available for LLM prompts.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a database instance, When the migration is applied, Then Dialogs and Messages tables are created.
Given a dialog record, When queried, Then the last N messages are retrieved chronologically.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Efficient retrieval of chat history for context generation
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease query time for retrieving last 10 messages from 200ms to sub-50ms

slices.title: Define API Contract for AI Configuration
slices.jtbd: When implementing the API for this epic, I want a shared contract for configuring the AI persona and system prompt, so that frontend and backend build against the same schema.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the API spec, When inspected by the team, Then endpoints to update the AI system prompt are documented.
Given the spec, When generating client code, Then no manual adjustments are required.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Backend-frontend integration bottleneck for settings
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce frontend/backend integration errors for AI settings from 15% to 0%

slices.title: Implement AI Configuration API Backend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want endpoints to update the AI persona system prompt, so that administrators can configure the behavior dynamically.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given an authenticated admin, When they send a new system prompt to the API, Then the backend updates the configuration in the database.
Given a new prompt configuration, When the LLM orchestration runs, Then the updated prompt is used.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Dynamic configuration of AI persona behavior
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease AI persona configuration update latency from 5 seconds to under 200ms

slices.title: Implement AI Configuration UI Frontend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the frontend for this epic, I want an admin UI to edit the AI system prompt, so that the AI persona can be adjusted easily.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the settings dashboard, When an admin accesses it, Then they can view the current system prompt.
Given the settings dashboard, When an admin edits and saves the prompt, Then the UI confirms the update successfully.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Admin experience in managing complex AI instructions
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce admin time to configure AI persona from 10 minutes to under 2 minutes

slices.title: Implement Inbound Message Listener
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want an event listener for incoming Telegram messages, so that new replies are captured and saved to the database.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given an active Telegram session, When an inbound message is received, Then the listener triggers an event.
Given the triggered event, When processed, Then the message is saved to the Messages table and linked to the correct Dialog.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Event-driven architecture and message deduplication
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Increase inbound message capture rate from 90% to 100% with zero duplication

slices.title: Implement LLM Orchestration Service
slices.jtbd: When implementing AI logic for this epic, I want a service that sends chat history to the LLM and processes its response based on persona rules, so that replies are contextually accurate.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given an inbound message event, When the service queries the LLM with chat history and system prompt, Then it returns a valid response string.
Given a lead with a profile description, When generating the first message, Then the LLM creates a personalized opening sentence.
slices.tocConstraintRef: LLM latency and prompt adherence
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease average LLM response latency from 10 seconds to under 3 seconds

slices.title: Implement AI Stop-Action and Handover Logic
slices.jtbd: When implementing workflow logic for this epic, I want to evaluate intents and message counts to pause AI and flag for human review, so that critical leads are not mishandled.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given an AI response indicating readiness for a call, When the engine evaluates the intent, Then the dialog status changes to 'Awaits human' and AI is paused.
Given a dialog with 5 consecutive AI messages, When the threshold is reached, Then the AI is frozen and flagged for operator review.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Algorithmic decision boundaries for AI autonomy
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Increase correct manual state transitions upon hitting safety thresholds from 80% to 100%

epics.title: Live Chat CRM
epics.jtbd: When monitoring outreach, I want a unified chat interface combining all accounts, so that I can seamlessly view conversation states and manually intercept critical dialogues.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease time for an operator to identify and respond to critical leads from 5 minutes to under 30 seconds
epics.tocConstraintRef: Fragmented visibility across multiple active Telegram sessions
requirements: R1: Unified interface for incoming messages from all accounts.
R2: Color-coded dialog states (In progress by AI, Needs human attention, Completed, Spam).
R3: Manual override capability that instantly disables AI for that specific chat.

slices.title: Define API Contract for Live Chat CRM
slices.jtbd: When implementing the API for this epic, I want REST/WebSocket endpoints for fetching chats and sending manual messages, so that the frontend can build a reactive UI.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the API spec, When reviewed, Then endpoints for polling/streaming messages and sending an operator reply are defined.
Given the spec, When used for implementation, Then backend and frontend data models match perfectly.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Real-time state synchronization contract
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce discrepancies in message DTOs from 10% to 0%

slices.title: Implement Live Chat API Backend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the backend for this epic, I want endpoints to serve chat histories and accept manual operator messages that pause the AI, so that the CRM is fully functional.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a request for dialogs, When the API is called, Then it returns the list grouped by state (AI, Human, Spam).
Given a manual message submission from the API, When processed, Then the backend sends the message via Telegram and updates the dialog state to disable AI.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Concurrency and state management when pausing AI
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease API response time for chat history from 1000ms to under 200ms

slices.title: Implement Unified Chat Interface Frontend
slices.jtbd: When implementing the frontend for this epic, I want a reactive Svelte interface to display color-coded chats and allow sending messages, so that operators can manage leads efficiently.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the chat dashboard, When loaded, Then dialogs are displayed with correct color markers (e.g., red for 'Needs attention').
Given an active chat, When the operator types and sends a message, Then it appears in the thread and the AI status badge indicates it is disabled.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Operator UI responsiveness and cognitive load
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Decrease UI render time for new messages from 500ms to under 50ms

epics.title: System Infrastructure and Deployment
epics.jtbd: When deploying the application, I want a containerized environment with Spring Boot, Svelte, PostgreSQL, and Redis, so that the platform runs reliably on a VPS.
epics.sixSigmaMetric: Reduce deployment time from 2 hours manual setup to under 10 minutes automated setup
epics.tocConstraintRef: VPS environment configuration and dependency management
requirements: R1: Deploy on VPS using Docker-compose (Spring App, Svelte SSR, PostgreSQL, Redis).

slices.title: Create Docker-compose Infrastructure Configuration
slices.jtbd: When implementing the infrastructure for this epic, I want a docker-compose setup for all services, so that deployment to the VPS is automated.
slices.acceptanceCriteria:
Given a fresh VPS environment, When the docker-compose up command is run, Then Spring Boot, Svelte, PostgreSQL, and Redis containers start successfully.
Given running containers, When service health checks are executed, Then all services report healthy status.
slices.tocConstraintRef: Container orchestration and networking
slices.sixSigmaMetric: Increase service uptime immediately after deployment from 80% to 99.9%
